demos.paper_text: This paper analyzes the impact of Maximal Extractable Value (MEV) attacks on decentralized networks utilizing Proof of Stake. We propose a novel decentralized sequencer architecture combined with encrypted mempools to mitigate transaction reordering and front-running.
demos.proposed_countermeasure: A novel decentralized sequencer architecture combined with encrypted mempools to mitigate transaction reordering and front-running.

demos.paper_text: We present a comprehensive review of Eclipse attacks targeting peer-to-peer gossip networks within Proof of Work blockchains. Our findings suggest that increasing the outbound connection limit and implementing strict IP-binding rules severely degrades the attacker's success rate.
demos.proposed_countermeasure: Increasing the outbound connection limit and implementing strict IP-binding rules.

signature.instructions: Extracts security vulnerabilities, consensus mechanisms, and countermeasures from academic abstracts.

fields.description: The abstract or methodology section of the research paper.
The specific consensus protocol discussed (e.g., PoW, PoS, PBFT).
The type of attack or vulnerability (e.g., MEV, Sybil, Eclipse).
A brief 1-sentence summary of the proposed defense.
